title: GoCell Contract Descriptor
description: Defines a Contract, the cross-Cell boundary protocol. All interactions between L1+ Cells require a contract. The endpoints structure varies by kind (http, event, command, projection).

id.description: Unique contract identifier in the form {kind}.{domain-path}.{version}.

kind.description: Contract kind that determines the endpoint structure.

ownerCell.description: The cell that owns (provides) this contract. Defaults to the provider endpoint cell. May be omitted.

consistencyLevel.description: Consistency level for this contract. Must not exceed the provider cell's consistency level.

lifecycle.description: Lifecycle stage. Transitions are one-way: draft -> active -> deprecated.

endpoints.description: Provider and consumer endpoints. Structure depends on the contract kind.

schemaRefs.description: References to JSON Schema files for request/response or payload validation. Paths are relative to the contract.yaml directory.

replayable.description: Whether the event or projection supports replay. Required for kind=event and kind=projection.

idempotencyKey.description: Field used as the idempotency key. Required for kind=event.

codegen.description: Opt the contract into `gocell generate contract` output. When true, contractgen produces types_gen.go / iface_gen.go (and handler_gen.go for kind=http). Default false; will be removed in PR-4 when all contracts are migrated.

description.description: Human-readable description of the contract's purpose.

deprecatedAt.description: Date the contract entered the deprecated lifecycle stage (YYYY-MM-DD). Required when lifecycle=deprecated. Enforced by FMT-23.

type.description: Primitive type of the parameter. UUID parameters use type=string with format=uuid.

required.description: Whether the parameter is required. Omitted for path params (always required); default false for query params.

format.description: Optional format hint (e.g. uuid, date-time, int64).

minLength.description: Minimum string length accepted for this parameter. Required by FMT-25 for non-uuid string path/query params.

maxLength.description: Maximum string length accepted for this parameter. Required by FMT-25 for non-uuid string path/query params.

minimum.description: Minimum numeric value accepted for this parameter. Required by FMT-25 for integer and number path/query params.

maximum.description: Maximum numeric value accepted for this parameter. Required by FMT-25 for integer and number path/query params.

server.description: The cell or actor that serves this HTTP endpoint.

clients.description: Cells or actors that call this HTTP endpoint.

http.description: Transport metadata elevating method, path, parameters, and status semantics to first-class contract fields.

method.description: HTTP method served by this contract.

path.description: HTTP path template served by this contract.

pathParams.description: Typed declarations for placeholders in the path template. Keys must match every {name} in path (FMT-13).

queryParams.description: Typed declarations for query string parameters accepted by this endpoint.

successStatus.description: Expected success status for provider-driven verification.

noContent.description: Whether the success response must be bodyless.

responses.description: Optional map of HTTP error responses, keyed by status code. Value references a JSON Schema describing the error body.

ownership.description: Object-level authorization subject/resource paths. Required when auth.serviceOwned=true (schema if/then enforces; governance FMT-32 also enforces). DSL form oracle: metadata.OwnershipPathValid; FMT-32 enforces path.<param>.* referential integrity against pathParams.

subjectPath.description: Caller subject path expr (ctx.*/path.*). DSL form oracle: metadata.OwnershipPathValid; FMT-32 enforces.

resourcePath.description: Resource owner path expr. path.<param>.* param referential integrity is enforced by FMT-32 against pathParams.

auth.description: Route-level authentication overrides for contractgen. Omit for standard authenticated routes. Mutex enforced by value-true (if/then const:true) so explicit `false` is equivalent to omission and never triggers conflict — see metadata.AuthComboLegal for the single oracle.

public.description: If true, route is mounted as public (no JWT required). auth.Route{Public: true} is emitted by RegisterRoutes. Mutually exclusive with passwordResetExempt, bootstrap, serviceOwned, and clientsOnly.

passwordResetExempt.description: If true, route bypasses the force-password-reset gate. auth.Route{PasswordResetExempt: true} is emitted. Mutually exclusive with public, bootstrap, and clientsOnly. May combine with serviceOwned.

serviceOwned.description: If true, listener JWT authentication still applies but generated handlers omit route-level Policy because ownership authorization is enforced inside the service. May be combined with passwordResetExempt. Mutually exclusive with public, bootstrap, and clientsOnly.

bootstrap.description: If true, contractgen generates a NewHandler(bootstrapAuth func(http.Handler) http.Handler, ...) constructor and emits auth.Route{BootstrapAuth: bootstrapAuth} from RegisterRoutes; the listener-level JWT middleware skips this route. Composition root injects the bootstrap Basic Auth + per-IP rate-limit middleware (GOCELL_BOOTSTRAP_ADMIN_USERNAME/PASSWORD env credentials). Mutually exclusive with public, passwordResetExempt, serviceOwned, and clientsOnly. FMT-28 limits bootstrap to setup/admin paths.

clientsOnly.description: If true, contractgen generates a single-arg NewHandler(svc Service) constructor and emits auth.Route without a Policy field. Authorization is provided solely by Contract.Clients caller-cell allowlist (auth.Mount auto-injects RequireCallerCell guard when Clients is non-empty). Mutually exclusive with public, bootstrap, passwordResetExempt, and serviceOwned. Requires endpoints.clients to be non-empty and path matching /internal/v1 or /internal/v1/...; FMT-28 enforces this cross-field shape.

responses.description: HTTP status codes injected by listener-mounted middleware (e.g. bootstrap auth 401, rate limiter 429). CH-04 governance treats these as declared without requiring handler AST emission.

allOf.description: auth.public=true is mutually exclusive with bootstrap, passwordResetExempt, serviceOwned, clientsOnly (value-true semantics; explicit false is fine).
auth.bootstrap=true is mutually exclusive with public, passwordResetExempt, serviceOwned, clientsOnly.
auth.passwordResetExempt=true is mutually exclusive with public, bootstrap, clientsOnly. May combine with serviceOwned.
auth.clientsOnly=true is mutually exclusive with public, bootstrap, passwordResetExempt, serviceOwned.
auth.serviceOwned=true is mutually exclusive with public, bootstrap, clientsOnly. May combine with passwordResetExempt.
When auth.serviceOwned=true, the ownership block is required. FMT-32 enforces path DSL and param referential integrity at the governance layer; schema enforces structural presence.

publisher.description: The cell that publishes this event.

subscribers.description: Cells or actors that subscribe to this event.

handler.description: The cell that handles this command.

invokers.description: Cells or actors that invoke this command.

provider.description: The cell that provides this projection.

readers.description: Cells or actors that read this projection.
